name: Tana River
description: Kenya's longest river at approximately 1,000 km, the Tana rises in the Aberdare Ranges and Mount Kenya and flows to the Indian Ocean. It is the major lifeline for Kenya's arid eastern region, used for hydroelectric power generation, irrigation, and recreation.

name: Ewaso Nyiro
description: The Ewaso Nyiro rises on Mount Kenya and flows roughly 700 km northeast through arid rangelands before draining into the Lorian Swamp, sustaining wildlife conservancies including Samburu and Buffalo Springs along its course.

name: Daua River
description: The Daua River rises in the Sidamo Highlands of Ethiopia and forms part of the Kenya-Ethiopia-Somalia border region before draining into the Somali Sea, flowing through Mandera County in Kenya's far northeast.

name: Athi-Galana-Sabaki
description: Rising in the Aberdare Ranges near Nairobi, this river changes names along its 390 km course — known as the Athi, then Galana as it passes through Tsavo National Park, and finally the Sabaki at its estuary on the Indian Ocean coast.

name: Mara River
description: Formed in Bomet County where the Nyangores and Amala rivers merge, the Mara River flows approximately 395 km through the Maasai Mara ecosystem — famous for the Great Wildebeest Migration — before draining into Lake Victoria as a tributary of the Nile.

name: Kerio River
description: The Kerio River rises in the Tugen Hills and flows roughly 350 km north through the dramatic Kerio Valley before draining into Lake Turkana, supporting communities along Kenya's central Rift Valley.

name: Turkwel River
description: Rising on Mount Elgon, the Turkwel River flows roughly 340 km through West Pokot and Turkana counties, feeding the Turkwel Gorge hydroelectric dam before draining into Lake Turkana.

name: Nzoia River
description: The Nzoia River rises in the Cherangani Hills and flows approximately 257 km through western Kenya's agricultural heartland before draining into Lake Victoria, supporting sugarcane and maize farming along its course.

name: Southern Ewaso Nyiro
description: Distinct from the northern Ewaso Nyiro, this 220 km river rises in the Mau Escarpment and flows south through Maasai country, draining into Lake Natron across the Tanzanian border.

name: Yala River
description: The Yala River rises on the Nandi Escarpment and flows roughly 219 km through western Kenya, feeding the ecologically important Yala Swamp before draining into Lake Victoria.

name: Gucha River
description: Rising in the Kiabonyoru Hills of Nyamira County, the Gucha River flows about 190 km through Kisii and Migori counties before draining into Lake Victoria.

name: Nyando River
description: The Nyando River rises in the Mau Escarpment and flows approximately 153 km through Kericho and Kisumu counties, draining into Lake Victoria near Kisumu city.
